50:
I remember crossing the border. Standing in Gyr Abania once more.
Me and my fellow Scions...the Warrior of Light among us.

I remember crossing the border. Standing in Gyr Abania once more.
Me and my fellow Scions...the Warrior of Light among us.

51:
Behind us, Baelsar's Wall, cutting the land in two.
Freedom on one side, tyranny on the other.

Behind us, Baelsar's Wall, cutting the land in two.
Freedom on one side, tyranny on the other.

52:
Tyranny...and the promise of a new beginning.
But one that could only be bought with flames and blood.

Tyranny...and the promise of a new beginning.
But one that could only be bought with flames and blood.

53:
I remember the old stories.
How the breaker of worlds came down from on high and cleft the earth with a single blow.

I remember the old stories.
How the breaker of worlds came down from on high and cleft the earth with a single blow.

54:
Rhalgr's Reach. A holy place, and a fitting home for the Resistance.
For the brave and the true, the loyal sons and daughters of Ala Mhigo.

Rhalgr's Reach. A holy place, and a fitting home for the Resistance.
For the brave and the true, the loyal sons and daughters of Ala Mhigo.

55:
They were waiting for us there. Men and women whose souls still cried out for what was lost.
For what could be ours again.

They were waiting for us there. Men and women whose souls still cried out for what was lost.
For what could be ours again.

62:
Ahhh, don't fret over that. You're not the first person to take up arms against the Empire under a false name. We'd do the same if we had any sense.
...My condolences for your loss, child.

Ahhh, don't fret over that. You're not the first person to take up arms against the Empire under a false name. We'd do the same if we had any sense.
...My condolences for your loss, child.

123:
People who had suffered every possible indignity.
Who had been treated like animals, day after day after day...

People who had suffered every possible indignity.
Who had been treated like animals, day after day after day...

129:
...Oh aye? A likely story! Only a fool would dare to come so late.
Or mayhap...a Resistance spy.

...Oh aye? A likely story! Only a fool would dare to come so late.
Or mayhap...a Resistance spy.

133:
...Is this a joke? You don't seriously expect us to believe this is a fifth?
How stupid do you think we are?

...Is this a joke? You don't seriously expect us to believe this is a fifth?
How stupid do you think we are?

141:
I'll bet the poor bastard can't believe his luck. He gets to go home.
We should do the same.

I'll bet the poor bastard can't believe his luck. He gets to go home.
We should do the same.

156:
Accordingly, we have extended an invitation to our neighbors.
Gyr Abania is where we shall host them─the field upon which we shall enjoy the sport of kings.

Accordingly, we have extended an invitation to our neighbors.
Gyr Abania is where we shall host them─the field upon which we shall enjoy the sport of kings.

157:
Patience is paramount. Cornered animals may have spirit, but they are ultimately predictable, and very poor sport. But if one dangles the promise of freedom before them, while nipping at their heels to stoke their passion, then things become interesting.
It is a delicate dance...and one which asks much of my hounds.

Patience is paramount. Cornered animals may have spirit, but they are ultimately predictable, and very poor sport. But if one dangles the promise of freedom before them, while nipping at their heels to stoke their passion, then things become interesting.
It is a delicate dance...and one which asks much of my hounds.

165:
Excellent... The disposition of the imperial forces is exactly as expected.
I will notify Father.

Excellent... The disposition of the imperial forces is exactly as expected.
I will notify Father.

170:
Too early to draw conclusions.
We must abort the assault and return to the Reach at once.

Too early to draw conclusions.
We must abort the assault and return to the Reach at once.

196:
The imperials were all over us before we realized what was happening.
No one knew where to run or what to do─it was chaos.

The imperials were all over us before we realized what was happening.
No one knew where to run or what to do─it was chaos.

210:
Your friends were a disappointment. But you...
You will entertain me, will you not?

Your friends were a disappointment. But you...
You will entertain me, will you not?

231:
Master Kemp too would be better served in the infirmary.
Let us take them there without delay.

Master Kemp too would be better served in the infirmary.
Let us take them there without delay.

245:
It'd be easy to curse them and call them traitors, but they're our children.
Our flesh and blood! If the only way to forge the future we want is to cut down our own, then...

It'd be easy to curse them and call them traitors, but they're our children.
Our flesh and blood! If the only way to forge the future we want is to cut down our own, then...

249:
Listen to the girl. We dare not suffer our comrades' sacrifices to have been
in vain. Now is the time to steel our resolve and press on, painful though
it may be.

Listen to the girl. We dare not suffer our comrades' sacrifices to have been
in vain. Now is the time to steel our resolve and press on, painful though
it may be.

251:
I still can't believe how strong he was. He humiliated us back there,
the Warrior of Light included. Gods help us if he's next in line to
the Garlean throne.

I still can't believe how strong he was. He humiliated us back there,
the Warrior of Light included. Gods help us if he's next in line to
the Garlean throne.

261:
For that, there would need to be someone left to fight on this front by the time you got back. Look, I'll not deny the plan has promise─and I feel for our brothers and sisters in Doma, truly I do─but I fear we lack the strength to
see it through.

For that, there would need to be someone left to fight on this front by the time you got back. Look, I'll not deny the plan has promise─and I feel for our brothers and sisters in Doma, truly I do─but I fear we lack the strength to
see it through.

269:
"Look ye where the sun doth rise, see crimson embers, dark'ning skies...
Look ye where the sun doth fall, see azure lost amidst the squall.“

"Look ye where the sun doth rise, see crimson embers, dark'ning skies...
Look ye where the sun doth fall, see azure lost amidst the squall.“

279:
A fine day, is it not? Fair winds and following seas.
The <Emphasis>Misery</Emphasis> is enjoying herself.

A fine day, is it not? Fair winds and following seas.
The <Emphasis>Misery</Emphasis> is enjoying herself.

287:
...Actually, might I persuade you to join me? Loath though I am to admit it,
I have a bad feeling about this.

...Actually, might I persuade you to join me? Loath though I am to admit it,
I have a bad feeling about this.

293:
All the colors of the rainbow, all the peoples of the world, all in one place.
I wish you could have seen it...

All the colors of the rainbow, all the peoples of the world, all in one place.
I wish you could have seen it...
